description: Tool-USAGE gold set: given the tool (fixed) + its description (full vs trimmed), does the model emit the CORRECT call (right params/values)? `type:call` checks params; `type:compute` checks a value the model must derive from the description (tests load-bearing how-to). The trimmed save_preview deliberately drops its coordinate-inversion formula.

cases.prompt: Save a preview of ONLY the active cel, with the coordinate gutter on, and also return the image inline.
Save a preview of the whole sprite at exactly 10x upscale, with NO gutter.

You saved a guttered preview at scale=16; the gutter's left band is 40px wide and the top band 32px tall. In the preview you spot a stray pixel at preview coordinate (296, 168). What is the SOURCE sprite pixel (x, y)? Return integers source_x and source_y.
Import walk.png as a 4-columns-by-1-row sprite-sheet, target 32x32 per frame, an auto 8-colour palette extracted by frequency, at 8 fps, tagged 'run'.
Import scaled_ref.png at 64x64, de-faking the scaled pixel grid first, snapped to the active palette.
Rotate the active selection clockwise 30 degrees, putting the result on a layer named 'Spin'.
Bayer-2 dither a rectangle at x=4 y=4 width=10 height=8 between #aa0000 and #ff8800, with 30% of the second colour.
Generate the wang16 (edge-only) autotile set from the 4 quarters I drew at source (0,0), tile size 8.
